title: Define an SLO and derive actionable alerts
description: Set a measurable service objective, compute an error budget, and create alerts that fire on budget burn rather than on every blip.
body: Steps: 1. Pick a user-centric SLI (e.g. fraction of requests under 300ms, success rate). 2. Set the SLO target and the resulting error budget over a window. 3. Define multi-window burn-rate alerts so fast burns page and slow burns ticket. 4. Make each alert link to a runbook and an owner. 5. Review against real traffic to tune thresholds. Rollback point: before paging policy changes. Validation: alerts fire on a simulated burn and stay quiet on normal noise; every alert maps to an action and owner. Risk boundary: an alert with no runbook or no owner is noise that erodes on-call trust — do not ship it; escalate alert fatigue rather than adding more pages.
